free_text: Sub-Coulomb proton induced reactions on copper

The compilation was approved by Prof. Laird,
Compiler comment: Thesis data were re-examined per
user's request. Serious issues with spin and parity
assignments for the ground states in 64,66Zn have
been found. In addition, gamma-ray transition
energies are listed as level energies. These errors
are detrimental for proton activation experiments.
Finally, the thesis results have not been published
according to NSR database. All these facts suggest
that thesis data should be flagged as potentially
deficient. These issues were rectified in corrected dat
BP: Re-examined and flagged these data as
(p,gamma) data sets with the corrected data from the
thesis advisor Prof. Chris Laird in subentries 2-8.
The neutron detector was the University of Kentucky
calibrated rabe and pube photoneutron sources, and by
measuring yield of previously calibrated targets..)
